title: Current-tyre finish pressure
summary: Estimate whether the current tyre can plausibly reach the race finish and how far it is beyond or inside its compound-specific life window.
rationale: The run repeatedly favored compact compound-window features over raw tyre age alone. Public-submitted variants also supported the idea that signed finish-margin features are more stable than a single old-tyre flag. Formula 1 strategy sources emphasize the trade-off between tyre durability and race distance.
implementation_hint: Within `preprocess(df)`, normalize `Compound`, estimate total laps and laps remaining from `LapNumber` and `RaceProgress`, map each compound to a conservative expected life, then add current tyre-to-finish age, signed finish margin, normalized finish pressure, and a current-tyre-cannot-finish indicator. Keep the original raw columns.
expected_effect: Medium expected ROC AUC gain with high confidence; this is one of the most stable observed feature families.
risk: Compound-life constants are approximate and may not transfer perfectly across races; clip extreme estimated race lengths and guard division by zero.
